1.author: Ahmed Ali
1.text: I CALL TO witness the early hours of morning,

1.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
1.text: By oath of the late morning,

1.author: Shakir
1.text: I swear by the early hours of the day,

2.author: Ahmed Ali
2.text: And the night when dark and still,

2.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
2.text: And by oath of the night when it covers,

2.author: Daryabadi
2.text: By the night when it darkeneth,

2.author: Hilali & Khan
2.text: And by the night when it is still (or darkens);

2.author: Itani
2.text: And the night as it settles.

2.author: Maududi
2.text: and by the night when it covers the world with peace:

2.author: Mubarakpuri
2.text: By the night when it darkens.

2.author: Pickthall
2.text: And by the night when it is stillest,

2.author: Qarai
2.text: and by the night when it is calm!

2.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
2.text: and by the night when it covers,

2.author: Saheeh International
2.text: And [by] the night when it covers with darkness,

2.author: Sarwar
2.text: and by the calm of night,

2.author: Shakir
2.text: And the night when it covers with darkness.

2.author: Wahiduddin Khan
2.text: and by the night when it darkens,

2.author: Yusuf Ali
2.text: And by the Night when it is still,-

3.author: Ahmed Ali
3.text: Your Lord has neither left you, nor despises you.

3.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
3.text: Your Lord has not forsaken you nor does He dislike you!

3.author: Arberry
3.text: Thy Lord has neither forsaken thee nor hates thee

3.author: Daryabadi
3.text: Thy Lord hath not forsaken thee, nor is He displeased.

3.author: Hilali & Khan
3.text: Your Lord (O Muhammad (Peace be upon him)) has neither forsaken you nor hated you.

3.author: Itani
3.text: Your Lord did not abandon you, nor did He forget.

3.author: Maududi
3.text: (O Prophet), your Lord has neither forsaken you, nor is He displeased.

3.author: Mubarakpuri
3.text: Your Lord has neither forsaken you nor hates you.

3.author: Pickthall
3.text: Thy Lord hath not forsaken thee nor doth He hate thee,

3.author: Qarai
3.text: Your Lord has neither forsaken you, nor is He displeased with you,

3.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
3.text: your Lord has not forsaken you (Prophet Muhammad), nor does He hate you.

3.author: Saheeh International
3.text: Your Lord has not taken leave of you, [O Muhammad], nor has He detested [you].

3.author: Sarwar
3.text: (Muhammad), your Lord has not abandoned you (by not sending you His revelation), nor is He displeased with you.

3.author: Shakir
3.text: Your Lord has not forsaken you, nor has He become displeased,

3.author: Wahiduddin Khan
3.text: your Lord has not forsaken you, nor is He displeased with you,

3.author: Yusuf Ali
3.text: Thy Guardian-Lord hath not forsaken thee, nor is He displeased.

4.author: Ahmed Ali
4.text: What is to come is better for you than what has gone before;

4.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
4.text: And indeed the latter is better for you than the former.

4.author: Arberry
4.text: and the Last shall be better for thee than the First.

4.author: Daryabadi
4.text: And the Hereafter is indeed better Unto thee than the life present.

4.author: Hilali & Khan
4.text: And indeed the Hereafter is better for you than the present (life of this world).

4.author: Itani
4.text: The Hereafter is better for you than the First.

4.author: Maududi
4.text: Indeed what is to come will be better for you than what has gone by.

4.author: Mubarakpuri
4.text: And indeed the Hereafter is better for you than the present.

4.author: Pickthall
4.text: And verily the latter portion will be better for thee than the former,

4.author: Qarai
4.text: and the Hereafter shall be better for you than the world.

4.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
4.text: The Last shall be better for you than the First.

4.author: Saheeh International
4.text: And the Hereafter is better for you than the first [life].

4.author: Sarwar
4.text: The reward in the next life will certainly be better for you than worldly gains.

4.author: Shakir
4.text: And surely what comes after is better for you than that which has gone before.

4.author: Wahiduddin Khan
4.text: and the Hereafter will indeed be better for you than the present life;

4.author: Yusuf Ali
4.text: And verily the Hereafter will be better for thee than the present.

5.author: Ahmed Ali
5.text: For your Lord will certainly give you, and you will be content.

5.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
5.text: And indeed your Lord will soon give you so much that you will be pleased. (Allah seeks to please the Holy Prophet – peace and blessings be upon him.)

5.author: Arberry
5.text: Thy Lord shall give thee, and thou shalt be satisfied.

5.author: Daryabadi
5.text: And presently thy Lord shall give Unto thee so that thou shalt be be well-pleased.

5.author: Hilali & Khan
5.text: And verily, your Lord will give you (all i.e. good) so that you shall be well-pleased.

5.author: Itani
5.text: And your Lord will give you, and you will be satisfied.

5.author: Maududi
5.text: Verily your Lord will soon give you so amply that you will be well-pleased.

5.author: Mubarakpuri
5.text: And verily, your Lord will give you so that you shall be well-pleased.

5.author: Pickthall
5.text: And verily thy Lord will give unto thee so that thou wilt be content.

5.author: Qarai
5.text: Soon your Lord will give you [that with which] you will be pleased.

5.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
5.text: Your Lord will give you, and you will be satisfied.

5.author: Saheeh International
5.text: And your Lord is going to give you, and you will be satisfied.

5.author: Sarwar
5.text: Your Lord will soon grant you sufficient favors to please you.

5.author: Shakir
5.text: And soon will your Lord give you so that you shall be well pleased.

5.author: Wahiduddin Khan
5.text: soon you will be gratified with what your Lord will give you.

5.author: Yusuf Ali
5.text: And soon will thy Guardian-Lord give thee (that wherewith) thou shalt be well-pleased.

6.author: Ahmed Ali
6.text: Did He not find you an orphan and take care of you?

6.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
6.text: Did He not find you an orphan, so provided you shelter?

6.author: Arberry
6.text: Did He not find thee an orphan, and shelter thee?

6.author: Daryabadi
6.text: Found He not thee an orphan, so He sheltered thee?

6.author: Hilali & Khan
6.text: Did He not find you (O Muhammad (Peace be upon him)) an orphan and gave you a refuge?

6.author: Itani
6.text: Did He not find you orphaned, and sheltered you?

6.author: Maududi
6.text: Did He not find you an orphan and then gave you shelter?

6.author: Mubarakpuri
6.text: Did He not find you an orphan and gave you a refuge

6.author: Pickthall
6.text: Did He not find thee an orphan and protect (thee)?

6.author: Qarai
6.text: Did He not find you an orphan, and shelter you?

6.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
6.text: Did He not find you an orphan and give you shelter?

6.author: Saheeh International
6.text: Did He not find you an orphan and give [you] refuge?

6.author: Sarwar
6.text: Did He not find you as an orphan and give you shelter?

6.author: Shakir
6.text: Did He not find you an orphan and give you shelter?

6.author: Wahiduddin Khan
6.text: Did He not find you orphaned and shelter you?

6.author: Yusuf Ali
6.text: Did He not find thee an orphan and give thee shelter (and care)?

7.author: Ahmed Ali
7.text: Did He not find you perplexed, and show you the way?

7.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
7.text: And found you deeply engrossed in His love, so directed you?

7.author: Arberry
7.text: Did He not find thee erring, and guide thee?

7.author: Daryabadi
7.text: And He found thee wandering, so He guided thee,

7.author: Hilali & Khan
7.text: And He found you unaware (of the Quran, its legal laws, and Prophethood, etc.) and guided you?

7.author: Itani
7.text: And found you wandering, and guided you.

7.author: Maududi
7.text: Did He not find you unaware of the Right Way, and then directed you to it?

7.author: Mubarakpuri
7.text: And He found you unaware and guided you

7.author: Pickthall
7.text: Did He not find thee wandering and direct (thee)?

7.author: Qarai
7.text: Did He not find you astray, and guide you?

7.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
7.text: Did He not find you a wanderer so He guided you?

7.author: Saheeh International
7.text: And He found you lost and guided [you],

7.author: Sarwar
7.text: Did He not find you wandering about and give you guidance?

7.author: Shakir
7.text: And find you lost (that is, unrecognized by men) and guide (them to you)?

7.author: Wahiduddin Khan
7.text: Did He not find you wandering, and give you guidance?

7.author: Yusuf Ali
7.text: And He found thee wandering, and He gave thee guidance.

8.author: Ahmed Ali
8.text: Did He not find you poor and enrich you?

8.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
8.text: And found you in need, so made you prosperous?

8.author: Arberry
8.text: Did He not find thee needy, and suffice thee?

8.author: Daryabadi
8.text: And He found thee destitute, so He enriched thee.

8.author: Hilali & Khan
8.text: And He found you poor, and made you rich (selfsufficient with selfcontentment, etc.)?

8.author: Itani
8.text: And found you in need, and enriched you?

8.author: Maududi
8.text: And did He not find you in want, and then enriched you?

8.author: Mubarakpuri
8.text: And He found you poor and made you rich

8.author: Pickthall
8.text: Did He not find thee destitute and enrich (thee)?

8.author: Qarai
8.text: Did He not find you needy, and enrich you?

8.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
8.text: Did He not find you poor and suffice you?

8.author: Saheeh International
8.text: And He found you poor and made [you] self-sufficient.

8.author: Sarwar
8.text: And did He not find you in need and make you rich?

8.author: Shakir
8.text: And find you in want and make you to be free from want?

8.author: Wahiduddin Khan
8.text: Did He not find you in want, and make you free from want?

8.author: Yusuf Ali
8.text: And He found thee in need, and made thee independent.

9.author: Ahmed Ali
9.text: So do not oppress the orphan,

9.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
9.text: Therefore do not oppress the orphan.

9.author: Arberry
9.text: As for the orphan, do not oppress him,

9.author: Daryabadi
9.text: Wherefore as to the orphan, be thou not Unto him overbearing.

9.author: Hilali & Khan
9.text: Therefore, treat not the orphan with oppression,

9.author: Itani
9.text: Therefore, do not mistreat the orphan.

9.author: Maududi
9.text: Therefore, be not harsh with the orphan;

9.author: Mubarakpuri
9.text: Therefore, treat not the orphan with oppression.

9.author: Qarai
9.text: So, as for the orphan, do not oppress him;

9.author: Saheeh International
9.text: So as for the orphan, do not oppress [him].

9.author: Shakir
9.text: Therefore, as for the orphan, do not oppress (him).

9.author: Wahiduddin Khan
9.text: Therefore do not treat the orphan with harshness,

9.author: Yusuf Ali
9.text: Therefore, treat not the orphan with harshness,

10.author: Ahmed Ali
10.text: And do not drive the beggar away,

10.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
10.text: And do not rebuke the beggar.

10.author: Arberry
10.text: and as for the beggar, scold him not;

10.author: Daryabadi
10.text: And as to the beggar, chide thou him not.

10.author: Maududi
10.text: and chide not him who asks,

10.author: Mubarakpuri
10.text: And repulse not the one who asks.

10.author: Pickthall
10.text: Therefor the beggar drive not away,

10.author: Qarai
10.text: and as for the beggar, do not chide him;

10.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
10.text: nor drive away the one who asks.

10.author: Saheeh International
10.text: And as for the petitioner, do not repel [him].

10.author: Sarwar
10.text: and do not reject the beggars

10.author: Shakir
10.text: And as for him who asks, do not chide (him),

10.author: Wahiduddin Khan
10.text: and do not chide the one who asks for help;

11.author: Ahmed Ali
11.text: And keep recounting the favours of your Lord.

11.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
11.text: And abundantly proclaim the favours of your Lord.

11.author: Arberry
11.text: and as for thy Lord's blessing, declare it.

11.author: Daryabadi
11.text: And as to the favour of thine Lord discourse thou thereof.

11.author: Hilali & Khan
11.text: And proclaim the Grace of your Lord (i.e. the Prophethood and all other Graces).

11.author: Itani
11.text: But proclaim the blessings of your Lord.

11.author: Maududi
11.text: and proclaim the bounty of your Lord.

11.author: Mubarakpuri
11.text: And proclaim the grace of your Lord.

11.author: Pickthall
11.text: Therefor of the bounty of thy Lord be thy discourse.

11.author: Qarai
11.text: and as for your Lord’s blessing, proclaim it!

11.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
11.text: But tell of the favors of your Lord!

11.author: Saheeh International
11.text: But as for the favor of your Lord, report [it].

11.author: Sarwar
11.text: and proclaim the bounties of your Lord.

11.author: Shakir
11.text: And as for the favor of your Lord, do announce (it).

11.author: Wahiduddin Khan
11.text: but proclaim the blessings of your Lord.

11.author: Yusuf Ali
11.text: But the bounty of the Lord - rehearse and proclaim!
